primary_sources.title: Joint resignation statement, Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, 17 May 2026
PH Deputy Chairman Anthony Loke statement, 17 May 2026: 'no necessity to have a by-election'
Federal Constitution Article 54(1) second proviso (two-year carve-out for vacant seats)

notes: On 17 May 2026 former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli and former Energy Transition and Natural Resources Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad announced they would quit PKR and vacate their seats (Pandan and Setiawangsa) to take over Parti Bersama Malaysia. PH Deputy Chairman Anthony Loke confirmed there is 'no necessity to have a by-election' under Article 54(1)'s second proviso (two-year carve-out window opened 19 December 2025). The sequence — resign-seat-first, leave-party-second — also sidesteps PKR's RM10 million party bond contractually but is constitutionally untested. The single most consequential intra-PKR rupture since the 2018 founding of PH. A second ElitePersonnelChange event covering Nik Nazmi specifically can be added once his actor record is created.
